title: eriogonum ammophilum
intro: Eriogonum ammophilum, also known as Sandhill Buckwheat, is a perennial wildflower native to the western regions of North America. This plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and prevent infections. The health benefits of Eriogonum ammophilum include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Eriogonum ammophilum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to support overall health and well-being.
